varieties.name: Toma Verde
varieties.traits: Golf-ball sized (2" diameter), bright green when young, turns pale golden-green when ripe. Papery tan husks. Plants 3-4 feet tall, bushy spreading habit.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, sweet-tart flavor like lemon-pineapple-tomato cross. Tangy and citrusy. Sticky coating when fresh.
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, 60-70 days maturity. Early maturing, prolific bearer.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Victoria, AU), Mr Fothergill's Australia, Local Seeds Australia, widely available

varieties.name: Verde Puebla
varieties.traits: 3-5cm diameter fruit, larger and more yellow-green than Toma Verde. Papery husks that dry and flake when ripe.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet/tart balanced flavor. Essential for authentic Mexican salsa verde. Full of flavor.
varieties.availability: Happy Valley Seeds Australia, The Seed Collection Australia

varieties.name: Rio Grande Verde
varieties.traits: LARGE apple-green fruits, 2-3" diameter (1.8-2.8 oz, up to 4 oz). Compact determinate plants 2-3 feet tall, medium-sized, no staking needed.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet and tangy, bright apple-green color. Sweet green pepper mixed with tomato flavor. Excellent for salsa verde.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields, quick maturing (80-85 days). Prolific producer.
varieties.availability: Available from various Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Purple Tomatillo
varieties.traits: Small-medium size (1.5-2" diameter), electric violet/deep purple color where exposed to sun. Golf ball shape, bushy plants 20-36" tall.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-tart flavor, sweeter than green varieties. Citrus notes of lemon with plum/pear sub-acid flavors. Less sticky than green types. Clear, delicate flesh.
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers, some Australian online retailers

varieties.name: Purple Coban
varieties.traits: Small fruits 1" diameter, green with dark purple hue that deepens at maturity. Compact plants.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, distinctive tomatillo flavor with citrus notes. Excellent for colorful salsas.
varieties.availability: Specialty heirloom seed suppliers, may require import

varieties.name: Purple de Milpa
varieties.origin: Mexico - grows wild in Mexican cornfields (milpas), ancient heirloom
varieties.traits: Very small fruits (¾" diameter), purple-tinged green. Plants 3-4 feet tall. Wild-type characteristics.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sharp, pronounced flavor preferred by some cooks. More intense than cultivated varieties. Mottled purple-green coloring.
varieties.yield_notes: 70-90 days. Fruit typically doesn't burst through husk when ripe.
varieties.availability: Specialty heirloom suppliers, Baker Creek-type seed companies

varieties.name: Gigante (Giant Green)
varieties.traits: LARGEST tomatillo variety - fruits 4" across (up to 10cm). Bright green. Sprawling indeterminate plants can reach 4 feet tall. Needs cages or staking.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweeter than most varieties (50% more sugar than standard types). Excellent flavor for salsa, soups, Latin dishes.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, 100 days maturity. Fruit falls when ripe.
varieties.availability: Burpee (may ship to Australia), specialty seed suppliers

varieties.name: Amarylla (Yellow Tomatillo)
varieties.traits: Golden-yellow fruits with peach-colored husks. Standard size. Cool-climate adapted.
varieties.flesh_fruit: SWEET rather than tart - unusual for tomatillos. Good for jams, jellies, preserves, fresh snacking. Sweet enough to use in cakes.
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, may require import from Northern Hemisphere

varieties.name: Pineapple Tomatillo (Ground Cherry type)
varieties.origin: Heirloom variety, sometimes classified as ground cherry (Physalis pruinosa)
varieties.traits: Small cherry-sized fruits, golden-yellow when ripe. Compact vining plants 18" tall. Yellow-gold fruit in papery husks.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Fruity pineapple flavor - sweet and tangy. Can be eaten green to yellow. Flavor improves as it ripens. Less tart than standard tomatillos.
varieties.availability: Specialty seed companies, heirloom suppliers

varieties.name: Mexican Strain
varieties.traits: Larger tomatillo, yellow when ripe. Traditional open-pollinated variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: More savory flavor profile than green types. Good culinary versatility.
varieties.availability: Territorial Seed Company and similar suppliers

varieties.name: Miltomate
varieties.traits: Small 1" diameter fruits, traditional highland variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Tart, sharp flavor - more intense than larger cultivated varieties.

varieties.name: Michoacan Purple
varieties.traits: Purple variety from Western Mexico. Regional characteristics.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-tart purple tomatillo flavor typical of purple types.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection Australia lists this variety

varieties.name: Plaza Latina Giant Green
varieties.origin: Seeds collected from imported Mexican fruit, Eugene Oregon 2005
varieties.traits: GIANT fruits 3-4" wide, oblate shape. Pale-green fruits, papery yellow-green husks. Super tall plants benefit from support. Less prone to dropping fruit than other varieties.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent culinary quality, described as 'Brandywine of tomatillos' - gourmet quality. Much higher value than small yellow varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: HUGE yields, 70-90 days. Most productive variety according to some growers.
varieties.availability: Adaptive Seeds, Thresh Seed Co. (may ship internationally)

varieties.name: Toma Verde
varieties.traits: Golf-ball sized (2" diameter), bright green, bushy 3-4 ft plants
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-tart, citrusy flavor. Excellent for tropical monsoon climates chutneys and curries alongside chili.
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, may be available through Indian agricultural networks. Seeds can be ordered internationally.

varieties.name: Verde Puebla
varieties.traits: 3-5cm diameter, yellow-green fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Balanced sweet/tart. Perfect for tropical monsoon climates-style tangy preparations and pickles.
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, Indian agricultural seed networks

varieties.name: Rio Grande Verde
varieties.traits: Large 2-3" fruits, compact 2-3 ft determinate plants
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-tangy, apple-green. Good for tropical monsoon climates curry bases and tangy gravies.
varieties.availability: International suppliers, Indian seed networks

varieties.name: Purple Tomatillo
varieties.traits: 1.5-2" purple fruits, compact 20-36" plants
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-tart, less sticky. Can be eaten fresh. Good for chutneys.
varieties.availability: International heirloom suppliers

varieties.name: Purple Coban
varieties.traits: Small 1" fruits, green with purple, compact
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, distinctive, citrusy. Excellent for pickles and preserves.

varieties.name: Purple de Milpa
varieties.traits: Very small ¾" fruits, 3-4 ft plants, wild-type
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sharp, intense flavor. Excellent for spicy tropical monsoon climates preparations.
varieties.availability: Heirloom seed companies internationally

varieties.name: Gigante (Giant Green)
varieties.traits: Largest 4" fruits, tall sprawling plants need support
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet (50% more sugar). Excellent for curries and dal.
varieties.availability: International suppliers

varieties.name: Plaza Latina Giant Green
varieties.traits: Giant 3-4" oblate fruits, very tall plants, less fruit drop
varieties.flesh_fruit: Gourmet quality, excellent flavor for tropical monsoon climates cuisine. High culinary value.
varieties.yield_notes: HUGE yields, 70-90 days. Most productive variety.
varieties.availability: International specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Pineapple Tomatillo
varieties.traits: Small cherry-sized, golden-yellow, compact 18" vining
varieties.flesh_fruit: Fruity pineapple flavor, sweet-tangy. Good for tropical monsoon climates-style chutneys and sweets.
varieties.availability: Heirloom seed companies

varieties.name: Mexican Strain
varieties.traits: Larger yellow fruits, traditional open-pollinated
varieties.flesh_fruit: Savory flavor, versatile. Good for tropical monsoon climates curries and stews.
varieties.availability: International suppliers, seed saving from initial purchase possible

varieties.name: Miltomate
varieties.traits: Small 1" fruits, highland variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Tart, intense, sharp. Perfect for spicy tropical monsoon climates dishes and pickles.

varieties.name: Amarylla (Yellow)
varieties.traits: Golden-yellow with peach husks, standard size
varieties.flesh_fruit: SWEET (unusual), good for jams and sweets. Suited for tropical monsoon climates dessert applications.
varieties.growing_notes: More resistant than tomatoes. Handles heat, drought, and diseases better than tomato relatives.
varieties.availability: International suppliers
